articles.title: Reliability of modified Radiographic Union Scale for Tibial Fractures (mRUST) Scores in Ballistic Lower Extremity Trauma.
articles.authors: Sun P, Eurich J, Zhang D, Schimizzi G, Christiano A, Erdman MK
articles.population: Patients with ballistic (gunshot wound) femoral or tibial shaft fractures treated with intramedullary nailing
articles.sample_size: 107 femoral fractures and 62 tibial fractures; 28 femoral and 25 tibial fractures in union prediction analysis
articles.outcomes: Inter-rater and intra-rater reliability of mRUST scores; sensitivity and specificity of 3-month mRUST scores in predicting nonunion at 9 months
articles.take_home: The mRUST scoring system is less reliable when used for ballistic fractures compared to blunt fractures. A low score at 3 months may help predict which femoral fractures will not heal by 9 months, but this prediction is less accurate for tibial fractures.

articles.title: Early Definitive Fixation and Risk of Deep Surgical-Site Infection After Tibial Plateau ORIF: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Le J, Xie K, Hershfeld B, Bitterman AD, Linn MS
articles.population: Patients with tibial plateau fractures (Schatzker I-VI and AO/OTA 41B-C)
articles.sample_size: 1,412 patients across 9 studies (668 early fixation, 582 delayed fixation)
articles.outcomes: Primary outcome: deep surgical-site infection. Secondary outcomes: superficial infection, overall infection, wound complications, and reoperation
articles.take_home: Fixing tibial plateau fractures within 72 hours of injury was associated with a lower risk of deep infection (8.7% versus 16.1%) compared with delayed fixation. Early fixation did not increase other complications and may reduce infection risk, though more research is needed to confirm this finding.

articles.title: Fragmented Sleep And Opioid Medication Utilization During Hospitalization Following Orthopaedic Trauma.
articles.authors: Marvin D, Hudson J, Ahn J, Schenker M, Und Aspang JS, Lewis J, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective analysis of a prospective randomised clinical trial
articles.population: Adult patients with isolated orthopaedic injuries treated surgically
articles.sample_size: 99 participants
articles.outcomes: Inpatient sleep patterns measured by wrist-worn actigraphy devices and association with opioid medication use measured in morphine milligram equivalents
articles.take_home: Patients hospitalised after orthopaedic surgery had short, fragmented sleep averaging 6 hours 36 minutes per day with frequent awakenings. Higher opioid doses were linked to even shorter sleep, with each additional unit of opioid medication associated with 2.7 minutes less sleep.

articles.title: Alarmingly High Rates of Complications and Refracture Among Patients with Early Periprosthetic Femoral Fracture Within 30 Days After THA.
articles.authors: Acuna AJ, Forlenza EM, Jones CM, DeBenedetti A, Terhune EB, Della Valle CJ
articles.study_type: Retrospective observational study using propensity-score matching
articles.population: Patients who underwent primary total hip arthroplasty between 2010 and 2022
articles.sample_size: 8,077 patients (1,725 with fracture 31-90 days, 1,887 with fracture 91 days-1 year, 4,465 with no fracture within 1 year, matched to early fracture cohort)
articles.outcomes: Rates of infection, refracture, and reoperation at 90 days and 2 years; 2-year survivorship free from complications
articles.take_home: Patients who had a hip fracture within 30 days after hip replacement surgery had much higher rates of further problems over 2 years, including re-fracture (42.6% had another fracture), infection (12.2%), and need for more surgery (51.5%), compared to those who did not have an early fracture or had fractures later on.

articles.title: Distal femur fractures: a comparison of hybrid and non-hybrid fixation of standard locking plates.
articles.authors: Plumarom Y, Singsuwit T, Marsh L, Phruetthiphat OA
articles.population: Patients with distal femur fractures treated with locking plate fixation
articles.sample_size: 90 patients with 90 fractures (26 hybrid, 64 non-hybrid)
articles.outcomes: Fracture healing progression measured by modified Radiological Union Scale for Tibia score at 6, 12, 24 weeks and final follow-up; union rates and complication rates
articles.take_home: Hybrid fixation of locking plates showed faster early callus formation at 6, 12 and 24 weeks compared with non-hybrid fixation. However, both approaches achieved similar final healing rates (100% and 97% respectively) with no difference in complications.

articles.title: Association between intraoperative fluoroscopy for anterior approach total hip arthroplasty and short-term revision risk: an analysis based on 49,878 cases from the Dutch Arthroplasty Register.
articles.authors: Vink MC, Zijlstra WP, Van Steenbergen LN, Van Veen PA, Doorn PF, Kuiper JWP, et al.
articles.study_type: Registry analysis with competing risk and multivariable analyses
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total hip arthroplasty via direct anterior approach
articles.sample_size: 49,878 cases
articles.outcomes: Revision surgery rates at 6 months, 1 year, 2 years and 2.5 years; hazard ratio for revision risk
articles.take_home: Using X-ray imaging during hip replacement surgery via the front approach was associated with a modestly lower risk of needing revision surgery in the short term (2.5 years), though revision rates were low in both groups and the clinical benefit was small.

articles.title: An Acta Orthopaedica educational article: Choosing treatment with an elderly patient having a distal radius fracture.
articles.authors: Landgren M, Navarro CM, Karjalainen T, Thorninger R, Hassellund S, Troest RW, et al.
articles.population: Elderly patients with distal radius fractures
articles.outcomes: Comparison of non-surgical versus surgical management approaches, radiological assessment, complication risks, and osteoporosis screening
articles.take_home: There is limited high-quality evidence comparing non-surgical and surgical treatment for distal radius fractures in elderly patients. Treatment choice should be individualised based on the patient's functional demands and specific needs rather than applying a universal approach.

articles.title: Effectiveness of magnets in removing metallic foreign bodies.
articles.authors: Aslanturk O, Ergen E, Karakaplan M, Ozdes HU, Al F, Ertem K
articles.population: Patients undergoing metallic foreign body removal surgery at a tertiary trauma centre between 2017 and 2025
articles.sample_size: 51 patients with 52 metallic foreign bodies
articles.outcomes: Success rate of magnetic extraction (87% overall), success rates by foreign body type and anatomical location, radiation exposure reduction
articles.take_home: Magnetic extraction successfully removed metallic foreign bodies in 87% of cases and may reduce radiation exposure. However, it worked better for splinters (100%) than needles (63%), and was more successful in the upper arm (100%) than lower leg (68%).

articles.title: The economic value and financial burden of informal caregiving during 3 months after geriatric fracture admission: A longitudinal cohort study.
articles.authors: Raats JH, Sluijter AJ, Lintmeijer L, van der Velde D, Schuijt HJ
articles.population: Working-age informal caregivers (under 67 years) of older adults (65 years or older) admitted to hospital with a fracture
articles.sample_size: 67 caregivers
articles.outcomes: Economic value of informal care per week (measured in euros), financial burden composite score (0-6 scale), and financial worry scores at baseline, 2 weeks, 4 weeks and 3 months post-discharge
articles.take_home: Informal caregiving for older adults after a fracture costs caregivers significantly more money during the first month after discharge, with weekly costs rising from around 92 euros before admission to over 200 euros in the weeks after hospital discharge. Despite this substantial economic burden, most caregivers reported little financial hardship or worry.

articles.title: The Effect of Bariatric Surgery on Complication Risk Following Total Hip Arthroplasty in Patients with Morbid Obesity.
articles.authors: Lex JR, Ekhtiari S, Entezari B, Koucheki R, McLawhorn AS, Pincus D, et al.
articles.population: Adults undergoing primary elective total hip arthroplasty, subclassified by morbid obesity status and prior bariatric surgery
articles.sample_size: 148,977 patients (10,287 with morbid obesity, 637 with prior bariatric surgery)
articles.outcomes: Major surgical complications within 1 year (revision arthroplasty, periprosthetic fracture, periprosthetic joint infection, dislocation), 30-day hospital readmission, and length of stay
articles.take_home: Patients who had bariatric surgery before hip replacement had higher rates of complications, particularly infections, compared with obese patients who did not have bariatric surgery. This suggests that delaying hip replacement to have weight loss surgery may not reduce the risk of problems after hip surgery.

articles.title: Glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist use for type 2 diabetes and risk of upper extremity fragility fracture.
articles.authors: Cathey JM, Fischer JP, Zhang J, Hammert WC, Klifto CS, Pidgeon TS
articles.population: Adults aged 50 years and over with type 2 diabetes on metformin
articles.sample_size: 64,232 patients after matching (32,116 in each group)
articles.outcomes: Upper extremity fragility fractures (distal radius, olecranon, proximal and distal humerus) over 5 years
articles.take_home: GLP-1 receptor agonist medicines do not appear to increase or decrease the risk of upper arm or forearm fractures in older adults with type 2 diabetes compared to standard diabetes treatment.

articles.title: Factors influencing implant failure in cephalomedullary nails for proximal femoral fractures: A retrospective review.
articles.authors: Moreau J, Frackelton R, Tun SMT, Abed R, Brunton L
articles.population: Adult patients with proximal femoral fractures treated with cephalomedullary nailing
articles.sample_size: 208 cases (9 implant failures)
articles.outcomes: Implant failure rate 4.3%; open reduction with cerclage wire augmentation associated with higher failure rate (10.5% vs 2.0%) compared with closed reduction alone
articles.take_home: Cephalomedullary nailing for hip fractures works well overall with a low failure rate. Patients who needed open surgery with wire reinforcement had more implant failures, though these were usually more complex fractures to begin with.

articles.title: Testosterone replacement therapy increases the risk of nonunion after surgical fixation of lower extremity fractures.
articles.authors: Smadi Z, Pereira DE, Mozny A, Irfan B, Boutros M, Hosseinzadeh P
articles.population: Adult male patients undergoing surgical fixation for lower extremity fractures
articles.sample_size: 9027 patients in each cohort after matching
articles.outcomes: Nonunion, revision surgery, deep vein thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, and wound complications at two years
articles.take_home: Men taking testosterone replacement therapy had a higher risk of fractures failing to heal properly after surgery on their legs, and were more likely to need further operations. Other serious complications like blood clots were not affected by testosterone therapy.

articles.title: Incisional Infiltration of Liposomal Bupivacaine in Geriatric Hip Fracture Surgery: A Randomized Parallel-Group Trial.
articles.authors: Yu X, Yuan Y, Zhao S, Sun Z, Han F, Wang S, et al.
articles.population: Older adults undergoing hip fracture surgery (hemiarthroplasty or total hip arthroplasty)
articles.sample_size: 76 patients, median age 77 years
articles.outcomes: Primary: resting pain at 48 hours. Secondary: pain with activity, opioid use, sleep quality, total sleep time, nocturnal awakenings, ambulation, length of hospital stay
articles.take_home: Liposomal bupivacaine injected into the surgical wound did not reduce resting pain at 48 hours after hip fracture surgery compared to standard pain management. Although it did improve some other pain measures and sleep quality, it did not help patients walk sooner or leave hospital earlier, so routine use is not recommended.

articles.title: Emergency department burden of e-scooter injuries in Victoria, Australia: A statewide analysis of injury patterns and prevention implications, 2022-2025.
articles.authors: Sharwood LN, Hayman J, Rezaei-Darzi E, Laska C, Eager D, Martiniuk A
articles.study_type: Retrospective statewide analysis of emergency department presentations
articles.population: People presenting to emergency departments in Victoria, Australia with e-scooter-related injuries
articles.sample_size: 4694 e-scooter-related emergency department presentations from January 2022 to December 2025
articles.outcomes: Annual presentation numbers, demographic characteristics, injury types (falls, fractures, head injuries), hospital admissions (38%), ambulance arrivals (approximately one-third), triage urgency levels, and seasonal variation
articles.take_home: E-scooter injuries are a growing problem in Victoria, with numbers increasing from 825 presentations in 2022 to over 1300 annually by 2024-2025, causing serious harm requiring hospital admission and emergency care. Most injuries happen when riders fall on roads or streets, with fractures and head injuries being common, and a concerning number involving children.

articles.title: Interprofessional collaboration in trauma care in South Asia: A scoping review of barriers, interventions, and implications for patient and system outcomes.
articles.authors: Kunju SA, Rahman AA, Mohammed CA, Krishnan S V, Soy EM, Patil DS, et al.
articles.study_type: Scoping review of barriers and interventions in trauma care
articles.population: Adult trauma patients in South Asian healthcare settings
articles.sample_size: 21 studies included in the review
articles.outcomes: Provider knowledge, confidence and communication; process reliability; patient mortality and functional recovery (infrequently reported)
articles.take_home: Educational and practice-based interventions to improve teamwork between healthcare professionals in trauma care consistently improved how staff worked together and the reliability of care processes. However, the review found that most studies did not measure whether these improvements actually reduced patient deaths or improved recovery, and organisational-level changes to trauma systems were rarely studied.

articles.title: Epidemiology and outcomes of geriatric patients with severe injury in Korea: Nationwide community-based study, 2016-2023.
articles.authors: Lee SY, Song KJ, Hong KJ, Park JH, Kim TH, Jeong J, et al.
articles.study_type: Cross-sectional observational study using nationwide database
articles.population: Geriatric patients aged 65 years and over with severe injuries treated by emergency medical services in Korea
articles.sample_size: 92,085 patients aged 65 and over (out of 319,567 total patients with severe injuries) between 2016 and 2023
articles.outcomes: Hospital mortality rates by age group; injury mechanisms and characteristics; predictors of mortality including age, sex, and injury type
articles.take_home: Older adults make up an increasing proportion of people with severe injuries in Korea, with the oldest patients (aged 85 and over) having the highest death rates in hospital. Falls are the most common cause of severe injury in older adults, whilst traffic accidents and suicide attempts carry higher mortality risk.

articles.title: The impact of urinary catheterization on urinary tract infection and renal function in elderly patients undergoing hip fracture surgery: A prospective randomized controlled trial.
articles.authors: Serttas MF, Uysal A, Degi̇rmenci̇ A, Gok U, Erten RA, Saglam F
articles.population: Elderly patients aged over 65 years undergoing hip fracture surgery
articles.sample_size: 150 patients (75 catheterised, 75 non-catheterised)
articles.outcomes: Postoperative urinary tract infection incidence and renal function (serum creatinine and urea levels)
articles.take_home: Using a urinary catheter during hip fracture surgery did not increase the risk of urinary tract infection compared to not using one. Patients without catheters had higher creatinine levels after surgery, suggesting catheters may help prevent kidney problems related to dehydration.

articles.title: Volar marginal fragment escape in distal radius fractures. Results of salvage with a volar open wedge articular reorientation osteotomy.
articles.authors: Gavaskar AS, Srinivasan P, Ayyadurai P, Tummala NC
articles.population: Adult patients with volar carpal instability from volar marginal fragment escape after distal radius fracture fixation
articles.sample_size: 21 patients
articles.outcomes: Osteotomy healing and radiocarpal stability restoration (primary); wrist range of motion, Mayo wrist score, and complications (secondary)
articles.take_home: Volar open wedge osteotomy successfully restored wrist stability and function in all 21 patients, with significant improvements in wrist bending and rotation ranges and overall wrist function scores at 23 months follow-up. Complications were minimal, though two patients developed arthritis and two had distal radioulnar joint instability.

articles.title: Analgesia for Kirschner wire removal in paediatric and adult populations: a narrative review.
articles.authors: Platt SR
articles.study_type: Narrative review of analgesic strategies for Kirschner wire removal
articles.population: Paediatric and adult patients undergoing Kirschner wire removal in office or outpatient settings
articles.sample_size: Multiple studies reviewed; specific sample sizes varied across included studies
articles.outcomes: Procedural pain scores, anxiety levels, patient satisfaction, and effectiveness of different analgesic and distraction strategies
articles.take_home: Routine pain relief medication is not needed for Kirschner wire removal in children or adults, as the procedure causes only mild pain. Simple distraction methods like tablet-based games work well, and if pain relief is needed, local anaesthesia techniques are more effective than general anaesthesia.

articles.title: Age-related macular degeneration and secondary fracture risk in patients with hip fractures.
articles.authors: Park M, Kim SH
articles.population: Patients with hip fractures from Korean National Health Insurance Service Senior Cohort
articles.sample_size: 18,611 patients with hip fractures (93 with age-related macular degeneration)
articles.outcomes: Secondary fractures (hip, wrist, humerus, spine, ankle, or pelvis) within 6 months after hip fracture surgery
articles.take_home: Patients with age-related macular degeneration had a significantly higher risk of secondary fractures after hip fracture surgery compared to those without the condition. This suggests that managing vision problems may be important in reducing fracture risk after hip surgery.

articles.title: Dual free flap reconstruction: A configuration-based algorithm using parallel and serial designs.
articles.authors: Ha Y, Kim JT, Hong SE, Kim YH
articles.study_type: Retrospective case series with algorithm development
articles.population: Patients with severe traumatic or complex soft-tissue defects requiring dual free-flap reconstruction
articles.sample_size: 32 patients at two centres (January 2015 to July 2025)
articles.outcomes: Partial flap loss occurred in 4 patients (12.5%); 28 patients (87.5%) recovered without major complications. Mean reconstructed area was 529.6 cm2.
articles.take_home: A configuration-based approach using either parallel or serial dual free-flap designs, chosen based on defect shape and blood vessel access, successfully reconstructed complex soft-tissue defects with most patients (87.5%) recovering without major problems.

articles.title: Biomechanical evaluation of locked versus non-locked proximal subchondral raft screws in a Schatzker Type II tibial plateau fracture model.
articles.authors: Steinhoff AK, Beyer RS, Chalmers CE, Zamorano D, Bui CNH, McGarry MH, et al.
articles.population: Fresh-frozen cadaveric tibias
articles.sample_size: 6 matched pairs (12 specimens)
articles.outcomes: Construct stiffness, fragment displacement, and load to failure during cyclic and stepwise loading
articles.take_home: Non-locking and locking screws performed similarly when stabilising this type of tibial plateau fracture, with non-locking screws actually showing greater stiffness during failure testing. This suggests that expensive locking technology may not be necessary for all patients with this fracture pattern, though locking screws remain useful in severe cases.
